name: Category Theory — Foundations
description: Category: objects, morphisms (arrows), composition (associative), identity morphisms. Functor: maps objects and morphisms, preserves composition and identity. Covariant vs contravariant.
mermaid:
graph TD
    D1["Def: Category\nC = Ob, Mor, comp, id"]
    D2["Def: Morphism\nf: A → B"]
    D3["Def: Composition\ng∘f when cod f = dom g"]
    D4["Def: Identity\nid_A: A → A"]
    D5["Def: Functor\nF: C → D preserves comp, id"]
    D6["Def: Contravariant\nF reverses arrow direction"]
    T1["Thm: Identity unique\nid_A ∘ f = f = f ∘ id_B"]
    T2["Thm: Functor preserves\nisos, monos, epis"]
    D1 --> D2
    D1 --> D3
    D1 --> D4
    D2 --> D3
    D3 --> T1
    D4 --> T1
    D1 --> D5
    D5 --> D6
    D5 --> T2
    classDef definition fill:#b197fc,color:#fff,stroke:#9775fa
    classDef theorem fill:#51cf66,color:#fff,stroke:#40c057
    class D1,D2,D3,D4,D5,D6 definition
    class T1,T2 theorem
